title: paeonia daurica nothosubsp. lagodechiana
intro: Paeonia daurica nothosubsp. lagodechiana, also known as Korean peony or Paeonia lagodechiana, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the peony family (Paeoniaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Korea. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Paeonia daurica nothosubsp. lagodechiana include reducing anxiety and stress, alleviating menstrual cramps, and acting as a natural remedy for respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tincture, decoction, or dried root powder for various health conditions, and it is also used in modern herbal preparations, including capsules and teas, to promote overall well-being.
